title: Symphyopappus catharinensis
taxonomy: Symphyopappus catharinensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the tribe Astereae and is native to Brazil. The genus Symphyopappus is a part of the subfamily Asteroideae. This plant is closely related to other species in the same family, which includes daisies, sunflowers, and lettuce. Further classification may include more specific details regarding its genus and species classification.

intro: Symphyopappus catharinensis is a plant species native to the subtropical regions of South America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against oxidative stress and cell damage. The bioactive constituents of S. catharinensis include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Herbal preparations made from S. catharinensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems.
